name: Hermetic memory palace: the ars memoria where a practitioner constructs an imaginary building and places vivid images in its rooms to encode knowledge. FSPLIT: the orator walks through the palace, splitting a unified argument into loci — each room holds one image encoding one point. FFUSE: walking the palace in reverse order reconstitutes the loci back into the complete argument for delivery. VINIT: the empty, unbuilt palace. TANCH: the palace walls — the boundary containing all memory. AFWD: walking forward (encoding). AREV: walking backward (retrieval). ENGAGR: the memory image is simultaneously the thing itself (vivid presence) and a symbol for something else — the paradox of mnemonic representation.

phase_0.domain_name: Hermetic memory palace: the ars memoria where a practitioner constructs an imaginary building and places vivid images in its rooms to encode knowledge. FSPLIT: the orator walks through the palace, splitting a unified argument into loci — each room holds one image encoding one point. FFUSE: walking the palace in reverse order reconstitutes the loci back into the complete argument for delivery. VINIT: the empty, unbuilt palace. TANCH: the palace walls — the boundary containing all memory. AFWD: walking forward (encoding). AREV: walking backward (retrieval). ENGAGR: the memory image is simultaneously the thing itself (vivid presence) and a symbol for something else — the paradox of mnemonic representation.

VINIT.justification: void before any memory is placed

AFWD.justification: encoding direction from entrance to final room

AREV.justification: retrieval direction from final room back to entrance

CLINK.justification: composition of loci into a chain

IMSCRIB.justification: the practitioner knows they are the one remembering

FSPLIT.justification: one argument branches into multiple room-images

FFUSE.justification: walking back fuses images into the original argument

ENGAGR.justification: simultaneously vivid presence and symbolic representation

IFIX.chosen_element: the engraved image in the room
IFIX.justification: permanent irreversible placement in the memory palace

phase_3.true_description: 01: a room holds a vivid image correctly
phase_3.false_description: 10: a room holds a wrong or faded image
phase_3.both_description: 11: the image is both itself and a symbol

steps.domain_action: Step 1 IMSCRIB: the orator declares 'I am the rememberer'
Step 2 AREV: the orator descends into the empty palace
Step 3 FSPLIT: the argument splits into distinct loci
Step 4 AFWD: the orator walks forward placing images
Step 5 FFUSE: walking backward fuses images into argument
Step 6 CLINK: the rooms are linked in sequence
Step 7 IFIX: each image is permanently engraved
Step 8 IMSCRIB: the orator recognizes the completed memory

phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: the linear path through the palace
phase_5.alfs_store: the first memory palace ever built

phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — images are placed without loss, retrieval is exact
